input.test_diff:
--- a/tests/test_diff.py
+++ b/tests/test_diff.py
@@ -45,3 +45,8 @@
+    def test_divergent_object_summary_format(self):
+        do = DivergentObject(id='x1', diffs={'name': ('a','b')})
+        s = do.summary()
+        assert 'x1' in s
+        assert 'name' in s
+        assert 'a' in s and 'b' in s

expected_top_finding: good string-format coverage — id + field + old + new all asserted
rationale: Real behavior test that would catch regressions in any of the four substrings.

input.test_diff:
--- a/tests/test_view.py
+++ b/tests/test_view.py
@@ -10,3 +10,7 @@
+    def test_view_objects_where(self):
+        # Pre-fix: View.objects(where=) raised TypeError
+        v = View(objects=[O(id='a', x=1), O(id='b', x=2)])
+        result = v.objects(where=lambda o: o.x > 1)
+        assert [o.id for o in result] == ['b']

expected_top_finding: reproduces the where= bug; assert is concrete and ordered
rationale: Bug repro test — pre-fix behavior documented in comment, post-fix assertion is concrete.
